extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won the bunch sprint in Balatonfüred, hitting the front at 300m which looked early but held off DEMARE and GAVIRIA.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step went front 1km out after Alpecin-Fenix led earlier, suggesting successful late-race positioning override early leadout investment.
Enkhorn took KOM points then stayed out solo 10s for several kilometers, revealing GC teams tolerate jersey-hunting solos before sprint organization.
Breakaway caught 28km out triggered immediate GC/sprint overlap positioning battle, indicating sprinters must contest positioning much earlier than sprint finale.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: Launched from 300m out which appeared premature but maintained speed through line, suggesting peak sprint endurance conditioning
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: neutral: Positioned well enough for second but couldn't generate sufficient late speed to challenge Cavendish's extended sprint
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: Podium finish and strong intermediate sprint performance confirms return to form after lean period
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: negative: Zabel doing leadout work but Nizzolo only fifth, suggesting Alpecin-Fenix sprint train mechanics not yet calibrated
rider_signals.MARECZKO Jakub: neutral: Fifth place for Alpecin's Italian sprinter shows depth but not dominance on home roads
